name: Ahti
description: Ahti, also known as Ahto, is a prominent figure in Finnish mythology, particularly associated with the sea and its depths. He is often depicted as a powerful sea god or a king of the sea, ruling over the aquatic realm and all its inhabitants. Ahti's domain encompasses not only the open ocean but also lakes, rivers, and other bodies of water. He is seen as both a benevolent and a potentially dangerous force, capable of providing sustenance and prosperity to those who respect him, but also of unleashing storms and destruction upon those who offend him. Fishermen and sailors would traditionally offer sacrifices and prayers to Ahti to ensure safe voyages and bountiful catches. He is often accompanied by his wife, Vellamo, the goddess of the sea, and together they maintain balance and order within their watery kingdom. Stories about Ahti often involve interactions with human heroes and shamans, who may seek his aid or challenge his authority. His character embodies the unpredictable and powerful nature of the sea itself.
subtitle: King of the Sea Depths
abilities: Control over the seas and weather
Bestowing good fortune upon sailors and fishermen
appearance: Ahti is often envisioned as a strong and imposing figure, with a long, flowing beard of seaweed and eyes that gleam like the depths of the ocean. He is sometimes depicted wearing a crown of seashells and adorned with pearls and other treasures from the sea. His skin may be described as having a bluish or greenish hue, reflecting his connection to the water. He can also take the form of various sea creatures, such as a large fish or a fearsome sea serpent.
habitat: Ahti resides in the depths of the sea, ruling over his underwater kingdom. His realm includes not only the open ocean but also lakes, rivers, and other bodies of water throughout Finland.
behavior: Ahti is a complex and often unpredictable deity. He can be generous and helpful to those who respect him and offer him proper reverence, granting them safe passage on the seas and abundant catches of fish. However, he can also be wrathful and vengeful, unleashing storms and other calamities upon those who displease him or disrespect his domain. He is a powerful force to be reckoned with, and his favor is highly sought after by those who depend on the sea for their livelihood.

keyMyths.title: Ahti and the Sampo
keyMyths.description: In some versions of the Kalevala, Ahti plays a role in the story of the Sampo, a magical artifact. He may be involved in stealing or attempting to control the Sampo, highlighting his ambition and connection to wealth.

sources.author: Elias Lönnrot
sources.description: The Finnish national epic, containing many stories and references to Ahti.
sources.text: The Finnish national epic, containing many stories and references to Ahti.

sources.author: Martti Haavio
